scope: Decomposes the SENEA price discount into attributable causes by tying each known event window to a testable claim, the evidence available, and an attribution assessment. Where a quantitative event-study return is required and the underlying market-data source has not been retrieved, the event is graded with status 'evidence-pending' and the exact data inputs needed are listed. No synthetic returns or inferred percentages are emitted.

newSourcesIntroduced.title: SENEA Class A daily price and volume series (event-window OHLCV) from a consolidated tape vendor
newSourcesIntroduced.publisher: NYSE consolidated tape / market-data vendor (e.g., Refinitiv, Bloomberg, Polygon, EODHD)
newSourcesIntroduced.urlOrCitation: Daily OHLCV for SENEA Class A across each event window listed in attribution-report.json (to be retrieved from a vendor that records consolidated NYSE prints; archive snapshot required for reproducibility)

newSourcesIntroduced.title: Small-cap and value factor return series (Russell 2000, Russell 2000 Value, S&P SmallCap 600, Fama-French SMB and HML)
newSourcesIntroduced.publisher: FTSE Russell / S&P Dow Jones Indices / Kenneth French Data Library
newSourcesIntroduced.urlOrCitation: FTSE Russell index history (https://www.lseg.com/en/ftse-russell), S&P DJI index history (https://www.spglobal.com/spdji/), Kenneth French Data Library (https://mba.tuck.dartmouth.edu/pages/faculty/ken.french/data_library.html)

newSourcesIntroduced.title: iShares Core S&P SmallCap ETF (IJR) and SPDR S&P 600 Small Cap ETF (SLY) holdings disclosures and rebalance flows around the July 2023 SmallCap 600 reconstitution
newSourcesIntroduced.publisher: BlackRock iShares / State Street Global Advisors / S&P Dow Jones Indices
newSourcesIntroduced.urlOrCitation: IJR and SLY daily holdings files (https://www.ishares.com/us/products/239774, https://www.ssga.com/us/en/intermediary/etfs/spdr-sp-600-small-cap-etf-sly), and the S&P DJI rebalance announcement archive

newSourcesIntroduced.title: U.S. Federal Reserve H.15 selected interest rates and Russell 2000 quarterly returns to define equity-market liquidity windows (2022 QT onset, 2023 SVB / regional-bank stress, 2025 macro window)
newSourcesIntroduced.publisher: U.S. Federal Reserve / FTSE Russell / Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (FRED)
newSourcesIntroduced.urlOrCitation: FRED series H15T10Y, DFF, DGS2, plus FTSE Russell return series; National Bureau of Economic Research recession dating where applicable

events.headline: S&P Dow Jones Indices announced removal of Seneca Foods (SENEA) from the S&P SmallCap 600 in the July 2023 rebalance.
events.claimTested: C-007 -- SENEA was deleted from the S&P SmallCap 600 in July 2023, producing forced selling pressure independent of fundamentals.
evidence: Plan Quick Source Anchors and claim C-007 in claims-source-pack.json identify the deletion (S-002).
Mechanism is forced selling by index-replicating ETFs: IJR (iShares Core S&P SmallCap) and SLY (SPDR S&P 600) must reweight to zero on the effective date (S-020).

Effective date and the bracketing trade prints, volume spike, and 1-/5-/20-day excess return vs. an S&P SmallCap 600 ex-SENEA benchmark have not been retrieved (S-018, S-020); this is open question Q-003 in the upstream pack.

attributionAssessment.rationale: The deletion mechanism is well-understood and the forced-selling sign is unambiguously negative for the deleted name on the effective date. The magnitude (event-day and event-window excess return, plus volume z-score) requires the market-data series in S-018 and the rebalance file in S-020 to compute; these have not been retrieved in this pass.

dataNeededIfMissing: Effective deletion date confirmed by the S&P DJI announcement archive (S-002).
SENEA Class A daily close, OHLC, and volume for [t-30, t+30] around the effective date (S-018).
S&P SmallCap 600 total return and IJR/SLY daily returns for the same window (S-019, S-020) to compute excess return.
IJR and SLY pre-/post-rebalance holdings deltas to size the absolute share-volume of forced selling (S-020).

events.eventType: Seneca quarterly and annual earnings release
events.headline: Seneca Foods quarterly (10-Q) and annual (10-K) earnings releases across FY2023-FY2025, including the FY2025 release that anchored the $359.3m LIFO reserve in the upstream pack.
events.claimTested: C-003 -- reported earnings materially understate FIFO earnings power because of a $359.3m LIFO reserve; C-011 -- pricing-cost recovery across cycles.
evidence: FY2025 LIFO reserve of $359.3m is anchored in claims-source-pack.json claim C-003 and financial-rebuild.json adjustedMetric fifo_inventory_adjustment_gross (S-001).

Reported pre-tax income, net earnings, operating income, D&A, and the prior-year LIFO reserve required to compute the FY2025 LIFO charge are graded 'pending' in financial-rebuild.json (open questions F-001 through F-003).

Without per-release reported EPS surprise data and same-day price reaction (S-018), the share of price drift attributable to earnings-release fundamentals (vs. mean-reversion or factor moves) cannot be isolated.

attributionAssessment.rationale: Earnings releases drive both the numerator (FIFO earnings power) and the market reaction. Until the per-release reported EPS, FIFO-adjusted EPS bridge, and same-day plus 5-day price reaction are computed, neither sign nor magnitude is anchored. The methodology is fully specified in financial-rebuild.json earningsPowerBridge but the inputs are pending.

dataNeededIfMissing: Filing dates of every 10-K and 10-Q released between 2022-04-01 and 2026-04-30 (S-001, S-007).
Reported pre-tax income, net earnings, operating income, D&A, and LIFO reserve per release (S-001, S-007).
Effective tax rate per FY for the after-tax FIFO add-back (financial-rebuild open question F-002).
SENEA Class A daily OHLCV around each release date (S-018) plus an industry-peer or S&P SmallCap 600 ex-SENEA benchmark for excess returns (S-019).

events.eventDate: FY2023-FY2025 buyback authorization and execution windows pending
events.headline: Seneca buyback authorizations and per-month repurchases disclosed in 10-K Item 5 / Item 7 and the FY2025 DEF 14A.
events.claimTested: C-008 -- Seneca has executed material share repurchases relative to float, supporting per-share compounding.
evidence: Buyback authorization, executions, average price, and net-buyback-as-percent-of-beginning-shares are explicitly defined as reportedMetrics in financial-rebuild.json but graded 'pending' (open question F-004).
Authorizations and 10b5-1 program announcements are press-release events and 8-K-disclosable; the exact dates have not been retrieved in this pass.
Same-day announcement returns require S-018.

attributionAssessment.rationale: Authorization announcements and executed repurchases are textbook positive shocks for tight-float, cash-generative names. Sign is positive but magnitude depends on the size of the authorization relative to market cap, the fraction executed, and the same-day reaction (S-018).

dataNeededIfMissing: Each 8-K or press-release announcing a new or expanded buyback authorization (S-001, S-005).
Monthly repurchase tables from FY2023-FY2025 10-Ks Item 5 (S-001, S-007).
SENEA Class A daily OHLCV around each announcement (S-018).
Beginning-of-fiscal-year diluted share counts to compute net-buyback-percent (S-001, financial-rebuild reportedMetric net_buyback_pct_beginning_shares).

events.eventDate: 2025-05 Del Monte Pacific deconsolidation effective date
events.eventType: Del Monte Pacific deconsolidation of U.S. Del Monte Foods
events.headline: Del Monte Pacific Limited classified the U.S. Del Monte Foods business as discontinued operations and deconsolidated effective May 2025.
events.claimTested: C-006 / IC-006 -- Del Monte Pacific deconsolidated U.S. Del Monte Foods, removing destructive private-label competition.
evidence: Pointer-level evidence anchored in claims-source-pack.json claim C-006 (S-003) and industry-bear-case.json IC-006 (status partially-verified).
Quoted text from the Del Monte Pacific FY2025 annual report discontinued-operations / deconsolidation paragraphs has not been archived in this pass (industry primary check P-005).
SENEA price reaction around the May 2025 announcement window has not been retrieved (S-018).

attributionAssessment.rationale: A deconsolidation by the parent of a destructive private-label competitor reduces expected long-run price competition for SENEA's private-label volumes. Sign is positive for SENEA. Magnitude requires (a) confirming the effective date and the announcement date(s) per S-003 and S-014, and (b) computing the same-day and 5-day SENEA reaction per S-018. Bear case B-003 (a leaner competitor may emerge from restructuring) caps the durable magnitude.

dataNeededIfMissing: Effective deconsolidation date and announcement date(s) from Del Monte Pacific FY2025 annual report and SGX/PSE filings (S-003).
Trade-press confirmation of the announcement window (S-014).
SENEA Class A daily OHLCV across [t-5, t+30] of each announcement (S-018).
S&P SmallCap 600 ex-SENEA or packaged-food peer benchmark for excess returns (S-019).

events.headline: Del Monte Foods (U.S.) filed for Chapter 11 in 2025, restructuring or removing a major branded canned-vegetable competitor.
events.claimTested: C-006 / IC-007 -- the U.S. Del Monte Chapter 11 filing removed or restructured a destructive private-label and branded competitor.
evidence: Anchored at the pointer level via PACER docket (S-008) and trade-press wire copy (S-014); status 'partially-verified' in industry-bear-case.json IC-007.
Filing entity (Del Monte Foods Inc. vs Del Monte Foods Holdings) and reorganization-vs-liquidation posture remain open question Q-004.
Bear case B-003 explicitly notes a Chapter 11 may produce a leaner competitor with reduced debt service rather than competitor exit.

attributionAssessment.rationale: First-order: a major branded competitor entering Chapter 11 reduces near-term private-label rivalry and creates take-or-pay co-pack opportunities, both supportive of SENEA. Magnitude depends on whether the case is a 363 sale to a strategic acquirer (bear case B-003 binds harder) or a long-tail liquidation. Same-day SENEA reaction must come from S-018.

dataNeededIfMissing: Exact PACER filing date and docket number for the Del Monte Foods U.S. Chapter 11 case (S-008).
Confirmation of filing entity (Del Monte Foods Inc. vs Del Monte Foods Holdings) (S-008, S-014).
Posture: reorganization plan vs Section 363 sale vs liquidation (S-008).
SENEA Class A daily OHLCV around the filing date and any subsequent material docket events (S-018).

events.eventType: Commodity and input-cost pressure (tinplate, steel, freight, energy, labor)
events.headline: Multi-year input-cost cycle: U.S. tinplate trade actions (Section 232 tariffs and AD/CVD duties), 2021-2023 freight inflation, and 2022-2024 labor inflation that pressure canning gross margin.
events.claimTested: C-011 / IC-008 -- pricing among remaining U.S. canned-vegetable producers is rational and cost moves are passed through; bear case B-005 -- tinplate / steel cost inflation outruns price recovery.
evidence: Industry-bear-case.json grades IC-008 'unverified' and B-005 'unverified' pending P-006 (FY2018-FY2025 revenue and gross-margin bridge from Seneca MD&A plus tinplate / freight indices).
Tinplate index series and the FRED canned-vegetable production index are registered as S-017.
Per-quarter SENEA price reaction to gross-margin surprises requires S-018.

attributionAssessment.rationale: Sign depends on pass-through. The bull frame (C-011, IC-008) treats Seneca as price-rational and cost-recovering, which would make commodity spikes net neutral over a cycle. The bear frame (B-005) treats incomplete pass-through as a margin compressor. Resolving requires the FY2018-FY2025 revenue and gross-margin bridge against tinplate / freight indices (P-006).

dataNeededIfMissing: Annual revenue, COGS, gross profit, and management-disclosed price/volume/mix commentary from FY2018-FY2025 10-K MD&A (S-001, S-007).
CRU Tinplate Index, S&P Platts tinplate spot, AAR rail and Cass freight indices, and FRED canned-vegetable production index for the same window (S-017).
U.S. trade-action history on tinplate (Section 232, AD/CVD timeline) (S-017).
SENEA Class A daily OHLCV around earnings releases that disclosed material gross-margin surprises (S-018).

events.eventType: Equity-market liquidity windows (Fed QT onset, 2023 regional-bank stress, 2025 macro window)
events.headline: Macro liquidity tightening windows that disproportionately weigh on small-cap, low-coverage, low-float names like SENEA.
events.claimTested: Implicit non-fundamental discount: small-cap-and-illiquid factor exposure widens the SENEA discount in tightening windows, narrows it in easing windows. Linked to bear cases B-002, B-007 and to claim C-009 (low coverage).
evidence: FRED-anchored Fed funds, 2-year, and 10-year Treasury series identify the QT onset (2022-Q1) and rate-cut pivot windows (S-021).
March 2023 SVB / regional-bank stress is a documented Russell 2000 drawdown window (S-019, S-021).
Russell 2000 / Russell 2000 Value relative-return series is required to attribute SENEA returns to small-cap and value factor moves vs idiosyncratic news (S-019).
Per-window SENEA returns and turnover have not been pulled (S-018).

attributionAssessment.rationale: Small-cap, low-coverage names with concentrated voting structures typically exhibit a higher discount in liquidity-stress windows. The sign is well-supported in the empirical literature. Magnitude requires running a return regression of SENEA daily returns on market, SMB, and HML factors across each window (S-018, S-019).

dataNeededIfMissing: FRED H15T10Y, DFF, DGS2 daily series (S-021).
Russell 2000, Russell 2000 Value, S&P SmallCap 600, and Fama-French SMB / HML daily returns (S-019).
SENEA Class A daily OHLCV across each window (S-018).
An NBER or analyst-defined window list to anchor the sub-period regressions (S-021).

events.eventType: Governance disclosure (dual-class voting power, insider beneficial ownership)
events.headline: FY2025 proxy statement disclosing combined Class A / Class B voting power held by founding-family and insider holders, plus board composition and related-party items.
events.claimTested: C-010 -- Seneca operates a dual-class share structure with concentrated insider/family voting control; bear case B-007 -- dual-class structure prevents activist crystallization.
evidence: Industry-bear-case.json grades B-007 'partially-verified', awaiting confirmation of combined voting power via P-009 (FY2025 DEF 14A, S-005). Upstream open question Q-006.
Plan Quick Source Anchors identify the FY2025 DEF 14A as the binding disclosure for the dual-class structure.
Governance-related disclosures rarely produce a same-day price reaction; the discount mechanism is structural (cap on activist crystallization) rather than event-driven.

attributionAssessment.rationale: Dual-class control structures persistently discount equity in event-study and cross-sectional academic work. Sign is negative-structural. Magnitude is rarely event-window-isolable; it shows up as a persistent multiple discount vs comparable single-class peers. Magnitude requires combined voting share (S-005) and a comparable-multiple panel of single-class packaged-food peers.

dataNeededIfMissing: FY2025 DEF 14A combined Class A + Class B voting share held by founding-family and insider holders (S-005).
Voting-rights ratio between Class A and Class B per the FY2025 DEF 14A (S-005).
A comparable-multiple panel of single-class packaged-food peers across FY2018-FY2025 to size the dual-class discount (financial-rebuild reportedMetric set, S-018).
Any 13D / 13G activist filings against SENEA across 2015-2026 that may have bracketed governance-event windows (SEC EDGAR).

events.headline: Documented absence of formal sell-side analyst coverage on SENEA, contributing to the discount via a coverage / neglected-stock premium.
events.claimTested: C-009 -- Seneca has minimal sell-side analyst coverage, contributing to the discount.
evidence: Anchored via S-006 (Seneca IR coverage page and consensus-estimate vendor records) in the claims-source-pack.
Empirically, neglected-stock premiums are documented in the academic literature on small-cap and micro-cap value.
Per-window SENEA bid-ask spread and turnover would size the liquidity component but require S-018.

attributionAssessment.rationale: Coverage absence is well-anchored at the pointer level and aligns with the academic neglected-stock premium. Sign is negative-structural. Magnitude requires (a) a coverage-count snapshot and (b) bid-ask / turnover series from S-018.

dataNeededIfMissing: Active-coverage analyst count per year from Seneca IR and a consensus-estimate vendor (S-006).
SENEA Class A bid-ask spread and average daily turnover by year (S-018).
A peer panel of similarly sized packaged-food names with at least three covering analysts to size the relative discount (S-018).

events.headline: Small-cap and value factor underperformance during 2022-2024 created a category-level headwind that persists through 2026-Q1; partial mean-reversion in 2025.
events.claimTested: Implicit factor-attribution: a non-trivial fraction of SENEA's discount is small-cap and value beta, not idiosyncratic.
evidence: Russell 2000 vs S&P 500 and Russell 2000 Value vs Russell 2000 Growth relative-return series document the 2022-2024 drawdown window (S-019).
Liquidity-window context (E-007) overlaps materially with this factor window.
Per-window SENEA factor loadings have not been estimated (S-018, S-019).

attributionAssessment.rationale: Sign is well-supported by the factor-return record. Magnitude attributable to SENEA specifically requires a Fama-French 3-factor or 5-factor regression on SENEA daily returns across the analysis window (S-018, S-019). Care must be taken not to double-count between E-007 (liquidity windows) and E-010 (factor context) -- the two should be combined as a single 'liquidity-and-factor' bucket in the verdict.

dataNeededIfMissing: Russell 2000, Russell 2000 Value, Russell 2000 Growth, and S&P 500 daily total returns (S-019).
Fama-French 3-factor or 5-factor daily series (S-019).
SENEA Class A daily OHLCV (S-018).
A regression specification: SENEA excess return = alpha + beta_mkt * MktRf + beta_smb * SMB + beta_hml * HML + epsilon, with sub-period coefficient stability checks.

events.eventType: Trading liquidity and float utilization (turnover, bid-ask spread, days-to-cover)
events.headline: SENEA Class A trades thinly relative to comparably-sized packaged-food peers, with elevated bid-ask spreads and concentrated insider/family ownership compressing free float.
events.claimTested: Bear case B-007 (governance) and claim C-009 (coverage) imply structurally low liquidity; this event captures the liquidity attribution distinct from the index-deletion shock (E-001) and the macro liquidity windows (E-007).
evidence: FY2025 DEF 14A insider beneficial ownership (S-005) defines the free-float ceiling.
SENEA Class A daily turnover and quoted spread series have not been retrieved (S-018).
Index deletion (E-001) further compressed institutional ownership, which is a sub-component of this attribution.

attributionAssessment.rationale: Liquidity is a documented driver of cross-sectional discount in small-cap names. Sign is negative-structural. Magnitude requires turnover and spread series from S-018 plus an institutional-ownership panel (13F-derived) to compare pre- and post-deletion ownership concentration.

dataNeededIfMissing: SENEA Class A average daily turnover, bid-ask spread, and Amihud illiquidity by year (S-018).
Pre- and post-July-2023 institutional ownership counts and percent-of-float from 13F aggregates (SEC EDGAR).
Free-float estimate net of insider holdings (S-005).

attributionBuckets.definition: Forced selling or buying driven by index methodology rather than firm fundamentals.
attributionBuckets.rationale: S&P SmallCap 600 deletion is a one-time supply shock with a well-documented event-window depression and partial mean-reversion. Magnitude requires S-018 and S-020.

attributionBuckets.definition: Price moves driven by changes in disclosed earnings power, balance-sheet strength, capital returns, or guidance.
attributionBuckets.rationale: FIFO-adjusted earnings power is anchored at the LIFO-reserve level ($359.3m) but the bridge to per-release reactions awaits the financial-rebuild data set (open questions F-001 through F-004) and S-018.

attributionBuckets.definition: Price moves driven by changes in the competitive set: deconsolidation, bankruptcy, or strategic exit by competitors.
attributionBuckets.rationale: Del Monte Pacific deconsolidation (May 2025) and the Del Monte Foods (U.S.) Chapter 11 are positive-sign for SENEA at first order, with bear case B-003 capping the durable benefit.

attributionBuckets.definition: Price moves driven by input-cost cycles (raw produce, tinplate / steel, freight, energy, labor) and pass-through completeness.
attributionBuckets.rationale: Bull (IC-008, C-011) and bear (B-005) framings disagree on pass-through. Resolving requires the FY2018-FY2025 revenue / gross-margin bridge against tinplate and freight indices (industry primary check P-006).

attributionBuckets.definition: Persistent discount driven by low free float, low turnover, wide spreads, and absence of sell-side coverage.
attributionBuckets.rationale: Both coverage absence (E-009, anchored via S-006) and liquidity (E-011) are persistent rather than event-driven. They show up as a multiple discount; magnitude requires turnover, spread, and a comparable-coverage peer panel.

attributionBuckets.definition: Macro liquidity tightening windows and small-cap / value factor moves that produce non-fundamental drift in SENEA.
attributionBuckets.rationale: 2022-Q1 QT onset, 2023 SVB stress, and the 2022-2024 small-cap-value drawdown each contributed beta-level downside that overlaps materially with E-007 and E-010. These two events should be combined as a single bucket in any final variance decomposition to avoid double-counting.

attributionBuckets.definition: Persistent discount driven by dual-class voting structure, insider control, and the resulting cap on activist crystallization.
attributionBuckets.rationale: Dual-class control consistently produces a multiple discount in academic studies. Magnitude requires combined voting power per the FY2025 DEF 14A (S-005) and a single-class peer panel.

verdict.summary: The SENEA discount is best modeled as a sum of (a) a one-time index-deletion supply shock with partial mean-reversion (E-001), (b) a structural liquidity-and-coverage discount (E-009, E-011), (c) a structural governance discount tied to the dual-class structure (E-008), (d) a beta-driven liquidity-and-factor drag through 2022-2024 with partial recovery in 2025 (E-007, E-010), and (e) two competitor-restructuring positives that have not yet repriced (E-004, E-005). Fundamental repricing on FIFO earnings power (E-002, E-003) and the commodity / cost-recovery bridge (E-006) are methodologically defined but quantitatively pending. No event-window excess returns are reported in this artifact because the underlying market-data series (S-018) and rebalance-flow files (S-020) have not been retrieved; inventing returns in their place would violate the acceptance criterion.
verdict.marketDataLimitation: All eleven events are graded magnitudeStatus=evidence-pending because S-018 (SENEA Class A daily OHLCV), S-019 (small-cap and factor return series), S-020 (IJR/SLY rebalance files), and S-021 (FRED rate / liquidity series) have not been retrieved in this pass. Per the acceptance criterion, exact data needed to compute event-window excess returns and factor-loaded attributions is enumerated in each event's dataNeededIfMissing field rather than inferred.

residualRisks.description: Effective date of the July 2023 SmallCap 600 deletion (carry-forward from upstream Q-003) is required to anchor the E-001 event window; without it, the index-deletion attribution cannot be sized.

Per-release reported pre-tax income, net earnings, and FY2024 LIFO reserve required to bridge FY2025 LIFO charge (carry-forward from financial-rebuild F-001 and F-003); without these, fundamental repricing in E-002 cannot be tested.
FY2025 effective tax rate (carry-forward from financial-rebuild F-002) governs the magnitude of the after-tax FIFO add-back; affects E-002 and the bear-case binding strength of B-010.
Buyback authorization and execution dates plus monthly repurchase tables (carry-forward from financial-rebuild F-004) required to anchor E-003 announcement-window analysis.

Filing entity and posture for the Del Monte Foods (U.S.) Chapter 11 case (carry-forward from upstream Q-004); reorganization-vs-liquidation determines the bear case B-003 binding strength against E-005.

Quoted text from the Del Monte Pacific FY2025 annual report discontinued-operations / deconsolidation paragraphs (industry primary check P-005) required to confirm the May 2025 effective date and announcement window for E-004.
Combined Class A / Class B voting power held by founding-family and insider holders (carry-forward from upstream Q-006 / industry P-009) required to size the governance discount in E-008.
FY2018-FY2025 revenue and gross-margin bridge versus tinplate / freight indices (carry-forward from industry primary check P-006) required to attribute commodity / cost pass-through in E-006.

Market-data series for SENEA Class A daily OHLCV (S-018) is the single largest dependency: every quantitative event-window or factor-regression magnitude depends on it. No event in this pass produces a numerical excess return without it.

Risk of double-counting between E-007 (liquidity windows) and E-010 (factor context). The verdict combines them into a single 'liquidity-and-factor' bucket; any final variance decomposition must use a joint regression rather than additive single-factor regressions.

Risk of attribution leakage between E-001 (index-deletion supply shock) and E-011 (structural liquidity / float). The deletion shock created a one-time level shift in institutional ownership that then persists as part of the structural liquidity discount; pre- and post-deletion 13F-aggregate ownership concentration is required to separate the two.
